name: Begonia dregei
aboutText: Begonia dregei is a fascinating caudiciform species, endemic to the rocky slopes and forest margins of eastern South Africa. It thrives in seasonally moist habitats, preferring bright, filtered light and well-drained, humus-rich substrates. This begonia develops a distinctive swollen, tuberous base and a multi-branched, shrubby habit. Its small, asymmetrical leaves, rounded to ovate with shallow lobing, are borne on slender, reddish petioles, contributing to its attractive bonsai-like appearance over time.

quickFacts.matureSize: Small, up to 30cm tall, forming a bonsai-like structure

morphology.leafShape: Asymmetrical, rounded to ovate with shallow lobing and crenate margins
morphology.growthHabit: Caudiciform with a swollen, tuberous base and shrubby, multibranched habit
